description:
Don’t miss the best places in  Marrakech Morocco 🇲🇦👇 We’ve now been travelling Morocco for 8 weeks and these are the places in Marrakech we genuinely think are worth your time. 🕌 Ben Youssef Madrasa- easily one of the most beautiful places we’ve seen in Morocco. Go early before the crowds arrive. 🏛️ El Badi Palace- quiet, underrated and somehow still overlooked by a lot of people visiting Marrakech. ⚱️ Saadian Tombs- smaller than we expected but the details inside are unreal. 🌿 Jardin Majorelle- touristy for a reason. First session in the morning is 100% worth it. ☕️ Bacha Coffee-  busy, overpriced and still somehow worth doing once 😂 Arrive EARLY. 🧺 The Souks & Spice Market - chaotic in the best way possible. Get lost on purpose. 🍲 Local restaurants- skip the main square food stalls and follow where the locals are eating instead. 🛏️ Stay in a riad- honestly, this completely changes the experience. Calm inside, chaos outside. Marrakech surprised us way more than we expected. It’s intense, beautiful, exhausting and addictive all at the same time. Which place would you visit first? 👇 Follow @steveandnes for more honest Morocco travel guides and places worth your trip 🌍 #marrakech #morocco  #marrakechthingstodo #marrakech🇲🇦morocco #marrakech_maroc_tiktok 
Discover Marrakech highlights: Ben Youssef Madrasa, El Badi Palace, Saadian Tombs, Jardin Majorelle, souks, riads and local eats. Go early and follow local tips. #marrakech #morocco
After eight weeks traveling Morocco, these Marrakech spots stood out as genuinely worth your time. Start early each day to beat the crowds and make the most of each site.

Ben Youssef Madrasa — easily one of the most beautiful places seen in Morocco. The courtyard and detailed tilework are exceptional. Visit first thing in the morning before crowds arrive to appreciate the architecture and quiet.

El Badi Palace — quiet and underrated. Many visitors overlook it, which leaves room to explore the ruins and open courtyards without the bustle. Its scale and history reward a slower pace.

Saadian Tombs — smaller than expected but with unreal interior details. The carved decoration and mosaics are compact yet richly detailed; allow time to take them in.

Jardin Majorelle — touristy for a reason. The morning’s first session is 100% worth it: colors, light, and cooler temperatures make the garden feel most alive early on.

Bacha Coffee — busy and overpriced, but worth doing once for the experience. Arrive early to avoid long waits and enjoy the atmosphere.

The Souks & Spice Market — chaotic in the best way. Get lost on purpose among narrow lanes, textiles, leatherworkers, and spice stalls. Wandering off the main paths leads to the most memorable finds.

Local restaurants — skip the main square food stalls and follow where locals eat. Neighborhood eateries offer more authentic flavors and better value.

Stay in a riad — staying in a traditional riad changes the Marrakech experience: calm, cool interiors and intimate courtyards provide a peaceful contrast to chaotic streets.

Marrakech surprised with intensity: beautiful, exhausting, and addictive. Which place would you visit first? Share your choice and follow for more honest Morocco travel guides and places worth your trip.

description:
📍Marrakesh, Morocco  If you’re planning a trip to Marrakesh, here are the top 10 things that you need to visit / try out 🤩 I got most of the attraction tickets on site as they couldn’t be pre booked online  Activities are booked through @GetYourGuide ! Get extra 5% off using ANTSDIARY295 💌 #marrakech #morocco #marrakech🇲🇦morocco #moroccotravel #fyp 
Discover the top 10 attractions to visit in Marrakesh, Morocco, and how to book your tickets! Get tips for an unforgettable experience. #marrakech #morocco #moroccotravel
Marrakesh, Morocco, is a vibrant city known for its rich history, stunning architecture, and bustling markets. If you're planning a visit, here are the top 10 must-visit places and experiences that you can't miss.

1. **Jemaa el-Fnaa**: This famous square is the heart of Marrakesh. It transforms into a lively marketplace by day and an entertainment hub by night, with musicians, dancers, and food vendors.

2. **Koutoubia Mosque**: As the largest mosque in Marrakesh, this iconic structure is a must-see. Its stunning minaret can be seen from various parts of the city.

3. **Majorelle Garden**: Created by the French painter Jacques Majorelle, this botanical garden is filled with exotic plants and vibrant blue buildings, making it a perfect spot for relaxation.

4. **Saadian Tombs**: Dating back to the 16th century, these tombs are a remarkable site, showcasing beautiful arches and intricate tilework.

5. **Bahia Palace**: This grand palace features stunning gardens and rooms adorned with intricate mosaics and carvings, reflecting the splendor of Moroccan architecture.

6. **Medina of Marrakesh**: The UNESCO-listed Medina is a maze of narrow streets filled with souks selling spices, textiles, and handmade crafts—perfect for shoppers!

7. **El Badi Palace**: Although in ruins, this former palace offers insight into the opulence that once was. Don't miss the stork nests on the walls!

8. **Marrakech Museum**: Housed in a former palace, this museum features Moroccan art, historical artifacts, and a tranquil courtyard.

9. **Atlas Mountains**: A short trip from Marrakesh, the Atlas Mountains offer breathtaking scenery and outdoor activities like hiking and exploring Berber villages.

10. **Traditional Hammam**: Experience a traditional Moroccan bath at a local hammam, a unique cultural experience that will leave you refreshed!

When planning your trip, it's important to note that most attraction tickets can be purchased on-site. However, you can also book activities through @GetYourGuide. Be sure to use the code ANTSDIARY295 to get an extra 5% off your bookings!

Exploring Marrakesh will not only immerse you in its rich culture and history but will also create unforgettable memories. So pack your bags and get ready for an amazing adventure in this enchanting city!

Lead
Our first night in Marrakesh was spent at El Fenn rooftop — an atmospheric spot highly recommended by travelers. The rooftop combines views, ambience and food, making it an easy choice for an opening evening in the city.

What to expect
Atmosphere: El Fenn rooftop delivers classic Marrakesh rooftop vibes: city views at sunset, warm lighting and relaxed seating that suits both couples and small groups. The setting feels special without being overly formal.

Food and drink
Menu highlights include Marrakesh-inspired dishes and a selection of drinks that pair well with the rooftop views. Portions and flavours fit the tourist-friendly, high-quality rooftop experience.

Why it's recommended
- Ideal for a first night in Marrakesh: convenient, scenic and welcoming. 
- Strong combination of view, service and food. 
- Good for photos and a memorable introduction to the city.

Tips for visitors
- Go at sunset for the best light and cooler temperatures. 
- Reserve ahead if possible — popular rooftop spot. 
- Bring a camera for rooftop city shots.

If you’re planning your first evening in Marrakesh, consider El Fenn rooftop for atmosphere, views and a solid food experience. Have you visited El Fenn or another rooftop in Marrakesh? Share which rooftop you’d recommend next.

description: 🇲🇦 15 things to do in Marrakech ⬇️ 🇲🇦➡️ Get my free Marrakech Travel Guide at marrakech.kelseyinlondon.com (link in bio) which includes:📍Places to visit ⭐️ Top things to do & activities 🍽️ Best restaurants 🏩 Hotel & Riad recommendations 📆 Best time to visit 🚕 How to get around⁣⁣ +  a saveable Google Map with all locations pinned 🗺️ 🇲🇦 15 things to do in Marrakech ⬇️ 1. Ben Youssef Medersa – This 16th-century Islamic college once housed 900 students and looks more like a palace thanks to its intricate carvings and tiled courtyard.  2. Souk Semmarine – The beating heart of the medina, packed with handwoven rugs, ceramics, brass lanterns and mountains of saffron.  3. Stay in a riad – Skip the big hotels and book a traditional riad for a more authentic experience.  4. Bacha Coffee – One of the most beautiful cafés in Marrakech, serving 200+ coffees and unreal pastries.  5. Go quad biking – Speed through palm groves and open plains, stopping for tea before heading back out. 6. Have a spa day – Marrakech is famous for its hammams. Book a steam bath and massage at your riad or opt for a spa package with transfers. 7. Medina Street Food Tour – Try pastille, msemen pancakes, olives, sweets and tagine. Arrive hungry – you’ll eat a lot! 8. Jardin Majorelle – These these iconic blue gardens were created by Jacques Majorelle and later owned by Yves Saint Laurent. 9. Agafay Desert – Spend a night glamping under the stars just an hour from Marrakech.  10. Marrakech Museum – Inside Dar Menebhi Palace, showcasing traditional and modern Moroccan art.  11. Le Jardin – Have lunch at this dreamy restaurant – make sure to order the pastille.  12. Jemaa el-Fnaa – The city’s buzzing main square filled with stalls, performers and street food. 13. Saadian Tombs – Hidden until 1917, home to Sultan Ahmad al-Mansur and 60 royals, with marble, gold and zellige tiles. 14. DarDar Rooftop – Sunset cocktails with DJ sets and medina views.  15. Atlas Mountains – Day trip to Ourika’s waterfalls, Berber villages, riverside tagine lunch and a women’s argan co-op. 🇲🇦 2 days in Marrakech Morocco | Things to do in Marrakech | Marrakech Restaurants | Marrakech Cafes | Marrakech Bars | Hotels in Marrakech | Marrakech food spots | Marrakech travel tips | Marrakech hidden gems | Marrakech activities | where to eat Marrakech. description: The ultimate 3-night girls trip to Marrakech ✨  Follow for more Marrakech travel advice 🐪  📍 Where to stay 🏡 Private villa - best if you want space and a pool 🕌 Or a riad in the Medina if you want to be within walking distance of everything  🗓️ Day 1 ☀️ Relax at your villa/riad 🌸 Perfume-making workshop at @Scenti  🍸 Rooftop drinks at Kabana 🍽️ Dinner at El Fenn 🗓️ Day 2 🛍️ Shopping in the Medina 🏺 Pottery shopping outside the Medina (worth the taxi for much better price and selection!)  🥗 Lunch: La Famille, Le Jardin or L’Mida 🧖‍♀️ Hammam & massage at Les Bains de Marrakech  👩‍🍳 Dinner back at your villa with a private chef (or head out to one of the restaurants below!) 🗓️ Day 3 🏊‍♀️ Pool day at Farasha Farmhouse or @Alkamar Camp Agafay القمر  🧳 Both offer luggage storage and changing facilities, making them perfect before an evening flight. 🍽️ More dinner recommendations ✨ Otto 🍝 La Trattoria 🌿 La Table du Riad 🚖 Transport tip Ask your riad or villa host to recommend a trusted private driver and pre-book your main journeys before you arrive. It's so much easier than trying to organise taxis on the day, especially if you're travelling as a group. 📌 Save this for your next girls trip, birthday or hen do 💅  #marrakech #girlstrip #hens #itinerary

description:
I have a new favorite perfume oil.. and I made it for $25 in Morocco 👀  this little shop is tucked away in the Souk Market in Marrakech and they had some of the most beautiful oils! 📍Perfume Studio, Souk Market, Marrakech  #customperfume #perfumeoil #moroccotravel #marrakechmarket #arabicperfumes 
Uncover the magic of a custom perfume oil I made for just $25 in Morocco's Souk Market! #customperfume #perfumeoil #moroccotravel #marrakechmarket #arabicperfumes
Marrakech’s vibrant Souk Market is a treasure trove of unique finds, and my latest gem is a stunning perfume oil I crafted for just $25! Nestled within the charming alleys of the market, I stumbled upon a little shop known for its exquisite selection of oils. The experience was nothing short of enchanting, as the air was filled with the intoxicating aromas of various scents.

At the Perfume Studio, expert merchants guided me through their remarkable collection, explaining the properties of each oil. They offered custom blending options, allowing me to create a fragrance that perfectly suited my personal taste. The charm of these artisanal oils is not only in their captivating scents but also in the stories each one carries from the heart of Moroccan culture.

What sets the Souk Market apart is its dedication to authenticity and craftsmanship. Unlike mass-produced perfumes, the oils here are made using traditional methods, ensuring that each bottle is a unique reflection of the region. If you're traveling to Morocco, this is an experience you won’t want to miss.

Whether you’re a perfume aficionado or simply looking for a memorable souvenir, the perfume oils found here can make for a special gift or a delightful indulgence for yourself. Next time you explore Marrakech, delve into the heart of the Souk and discover the stunning world of Moroccan perfumes that await you. 

Don’t forget to share your scent experiences from your travels and drop by this hidden gem during your visit!

description:
🌵 Une des meilleures activités qu’on a faites à Marrakech ! 🤩 Quad dans le désert d’Agafay 🏍️ au couché du soleil, balade, visite d’une coopérative d’huile D’ARGAN, Spectacle de feu, repas et transport aller-retour inclus 🚐… Le tout réservé sur GetYourGuide à un prix vraiment abordable. 💸  180MAD Une activité conviviale, parfaite à faire en famille, entre amis ou en couple. ❤️ Franchement, le rapport qualité/prix est incroyable, on recommande à 100 % ! 🇲🇦✨ 📍Désert d’Agafay – Marrakech 💛 Réservation via GetYourGuide #Marrakech #Agafay #Spectaclefeu #QuadMarrakech #GetYourGuide  
Experience an unforgettable quad biking adventure in Agafay Desert, Marrakech. Book now for an exciting sunset tour with dining and entertainment! #Marrakech #GetYourGuide
One of the best activities to experience in Marrakech is undoubtedly a quad biking adventure in the stunning Agafay Desert, especially during sunset. This exciting excursion offers a perfect blend of thrill and beauty as you race across the sandy terrain while the sun sets, painting the sky in vibrant hues. 

During this memorable tour, you'll not only enjoy the adrenaline rush of quad biking, but you'll also have the chance to visit a local cooperative specializing in argan oil production. This visit provides insights into the traditional methods of argan oil extraction, a vital part of Moroccan culture and economy. 

After your adventure, you'll be treated to a captivating fire show that adds a touch of magic to your evening. The entire experience is completed with a delicious meal and inclusive round-trip transportation, making it very convenient for families, friends, or couples looking for a unique outing. 

With an exceptional price of only 180 MAD, you can enjoy a friendly atmosphere and create lasting memories. The value for money is incredible—this adventure is highly recommended for anyone visiting Marrakech! 

Book your experience today through GetYourGuide and get ready for an unforgettable day in the Agafay Desert!

description:
5 day itinerary in January - fly with me to Morocco, Marrakech :) 🇲🇦 #marrakech #traveltok #fyp 
Explore an exciting 5-day itinerary in Marrakech this January. Get tips on your Morocco adventure and travel planning! #marrakech #traveltok
### Discovering the Magic of Marrakech: A 5-Day Itinerary 🇲🇦

If you're dreaming of a vibrant getaway, look no further than Marrakech, Morocco! In January, I embarked on a captivating 5-day adventure that turned out to be an unforgettable experience. Here's a detailed breakdown of my journey, including all the highlights that made my trip special. 

#### Day 1: Arrival and Exploring the City
I flew into Marrakech, and the total cost for flights and a centrally located Airbnb was just £220. What a steal for five fantastic days! 🌞 After settling in, my first exploration took me from the stunning Koutoubia Mosque to Jemaa el Fna, the main square buzzing with energy and life. Be prepared to haggle! It’s all part of the experience—but don’t worry, the fresh juice stalls with refreshments under £2 make it worthwhile. One of the must-visit spots is the Dardar Rooftop restaurant, where the sunset views paired with delicious cuisine are simply breathtaking; we indulged in a wonderful meal for only £19 each. 

#### Day 2: Gardens and Adventure
On my second day, I visited the enchanting Jardin Majorelle, a botanical paradise, which is conveniently next to the YSL Museum. As a student, I snagged a combined ticket for just £15! 🌼 Later, I was off to the Agafay Desert for some thrilling adventures. Riding camels, quad biking at sunset, and enjoying a delectable buffet with fire shows—all for £30—made the day unforgettable! 

#### Day 3: Thrills in the Atlas Mountains
Day three pushed my limits as I ventured to the Atlas Mountains. We zipped along a rope above stunning landscapes and ziplined from mountain to mountain, soaking in spectacular views. 🌄 This adrenaline-pumping experience cost £40 and led us to a lovely breakfast in a quaint valley. I also visited the Bahia Palace, known for its incredible architecture, and took a memorable horseback ride. 

#### Day 4: Waterfalls and Fine Dining
The beauty continued on day four when I took a £20 trip to mesmerizing waterfalls. I even got up close on a boat ride and met an adorable monkey! 🐒 To cap off the day, I indulged in a luxurious dinner at Nouba. The ambiance was fantastic, and while it was a bit pricier, it was worth a visit for that special experience—though perhaps not a must-do unless you're after some luxury vibes.

#### Day 5: A Stunning Sunrise
Waking up at 5:00 AM was so worth it for the hot air balloon ride over the Atlas Mountains. 🌅 While waiting, I enjoyed the company of friendly dogs. The view from above was extraordinary, despite the playful choice of background music! The brunch afterwards was an absolute 10/10, and while this was the most expensive activity at £99, it was truly a highlight. After a quick visit to the Saadian Tombs for £8, it was nearly time to head back to the UK.

#### Total Cost and Final Thoughts
In total, along with food and transport, my five-day Marrakech adventure came to about £650 each—a reasonable investment for such rich experiences. 

If you're looking for holiday ideas or travel inspiration, Marrakech is a wonderful option! Let me know where you’d like me to travel next, and perhaps you can join me on this travel journey! ✈️🌍

#Marrakech #TravelTok #HolidayIdeas #TravelHacks #AdventureAwaits

description: The relaxation you never knew you needed after exploring the busy streets of Marrakech 🇲🇦  We spent a blissful spa day at @Lesbainsdulotus , choosing the “Authentic Bath + 50 min Oriental Relaxation” -  a traditional Moroccan hammam followed by a soothing massage. It was exactly what we needed at the end of our almost three-week adventure through the beautiful chaos of the city. Completely rejuvenating! I honestly feel zen in every sense of the word. From start to finish, everything was perfectly organized, and we felt so well cared for. It’s hard to describe just how soothing and comforting the experience was after days of wandering Marrakech’s vibrant streets.  The price of the experience we had is 850 MAD (79 €) and it’s worth every penny! Thank you @lesbainsdulotus for such an incredible and restorative experience - the perfect way to end our trip. 💆‍♀️✨ 📍Les Bains Du Lotus, Marrakech 🇲🇦 | Marrakech | Things to do in Marrakech | Marrakech tips | Marrakech itinerary | Morocco | Morocco trip | Marrakech trip | Morocco itinerary | Morocco things to do | Moroccan hammam | places to visit in Marrakech

description:
Morocco has wayyy more to offer than camel rides 🇲🇦🔥 Waterkarting in Morocco! #waterkarting #marrakechitinerary #thingstodoinmarrakech #visitmorocco #moroccotravel  Waterkarting in Marrakech | Activities in Morocco
Explore the exciting world of waterkarting in Morocco and uncover the adventures waiting beyond camel rides! #waterkarting #marrakechitinerary #thingstodoinmarrakech #visitmorocco #moroccotravel
Morocco is often synonymous with camel rides across the desert, but there's a thrilling adventure waiting for you that goes beyond the traditional experience: waterkarting in Marrakech. This exciting activity invites both locals and tourists to explore the beautiful waterways while enjoying the exhilarating rush of speed on water.

Waterkarting is gaining popularity among adventure seekers visiting Morocco. This unique water activity allows you to glide across the water, providing an unforgettable experience that combines the beauty of Marrakech with the thrill of water sports. Whether you're looking for a family-friendly activity or an adrenaline-pumping adventure with friends, waterkarting offers the perfect blend of excitement and scenic views.

Set against the backdrop of the stunning landscapes of Marrakech, participants can enjoy a unique perspective of the area. Glide over the water, marvel at the natural beauty, and feel the rush of wind as you navigate through waterways often untouched by traditional tourist routes.

In addition to waterkarting, Morocco offers a plethora of activities that cater to various interests. From cultural experiences in the vibrant markets to hot air balloon rides over the stunning landscapes, visitors can fill their itineraries with incredible experiences that showcase Morocco's diverse offerings. 

If you're planning a trip to Marrakech, make sure to include waterkarting in your itinerary. It's not just about the camel rides anymore; it's about diving into new experiences and exploring all that Morocco has to offer. Don't miss out on this unique opportunity to add some exciting memories to your Moroccan adventure!

We spent three immersive days exploring every facet of Marrakech, from the vibrant souks and historic Medina to the serene Secret Garden and the opulent Bahia Palace. And of course, the delicious food was a highlight we won't soon forget.

This 3-day Marrakech itinerary is crafted from our day-by-day exploration, designed to guide you in planning your own unforgettable trip. description:
Marrakech is best experienced in a riad  #marrakech #riad #luxurytravels #morocco 
Discover why staying in a Marrakech riad offers authentic luxury and local charm—perfect for your Morocco travel plans. #marrakech #riad #luxurytravels #morocco
Marrakech is best experienced in a riad. A riad places you inside the medina’s winding streets while offering a quiet, luxurious retreat centered around a courtyard or garden. Choosing a riad means waking to birdsong, tiled fountains, and locally crafted décor—elements that reflect Moroccan design and daily life.

Why choose a riad in Marrakech
- Central location: Many riads sit within the old medina, putting souks, palaces, and historic sites within walking distance. This saves time and lets you explore narrow alleys that vehicles cannot reach.
- Authentic atmosphere: Riads preserve traditional architecture—inner courtyards, mosaic tilework, carved wood, and rooftop terraces—creating an intimate, culturally rooted stay.
- Personal service: Smaller than standard hotels, riads often provide attentive, personalized hospitality, from homemade breakfasts to tailored local recommendations.

What to expect in a riad
- Layout and amenities: Expect inward-facing rooms around a central courtyard, often with a fountain or plants. Many riads offer rooftop terraces with city views, private or shared lounges, and sometimes a small plunge pool.
- Food and dining: Breakfast is commonly included and may feature Moroccan staples like mint tea, fresh bread, and local pastries. Some riads provide evening meals on request—ideal for tasting home-cooked Moroccan cuisine.
- Atmosphere and privacy: Riads are typically quiet sanctuaries despite medina bustle outside. Rooms are designed for relaxation after days of sightseeing.

Tips for booking and staying
- Location matters: Verify the riad’s entrance is accessible and note proximity to major medina gates or landmarks to ease navigation.
- Read reviews: Look for mentions of cleanliness, service, and accuracy of photos. Pay attention to comments about noise or accessibility.
- Ask about amenities: Confirm if the riad has heating or air conditioning, Wi‑Fi, rooftop access, or transport arrangements to and from the airport.
- Respect house rules: Riads are often family-run; observe quiet hours and local customs.

Enhancing your Marrakech stay
Combine riad living with travel ideas like guided medina tours, visits to Jardin Majorelle, day trips to the Atlas Mountains, or food-focused strolls through souks. Use rooftop moments for sunset photos or relaxed evenings sipping mint tea.

Takeaway
For an immersive, comfortable, and characterful stay in Marrakech, a riad is the ideal choice. It blends authentic Moroccan architecture, personalized service, and a peaceful base for exploring. Planning ahead—location, reviews, and amenities—ensures the riad matches your travel style.

Would you prefer a riad near the main souks or a quieter riad deeper in the medina? Share your ideal riad feature and start planning your Morocco trip.

Lead
Villa 5 chambres familiale à louer près de Marrakech, équipée d’une grande piscine privée et conçue pour groupes et familles cherchant confort et proximité aux zones touristiques comme la Palmeraie, la route de l’Ourika ou l’aéroport.

Description générale
Cette villa familiale dispose de cinq chambres, d’un grand espace extérieur avec piscine privée, et d’installations adaptées aux séjours en famille ou en groupe. Emplacement idéal pour accès rapide à Marrakech et aux lieux populaires : Palmeraie, la médina et la place Jemaa el-Fna.

Services et options disponibles
- Piscine privée spacieuse pour détente et baignade.
- Possibilité de services additionnels : chef, aide ménagère (خدامة) selon demande.
- Options de location : quotidien, weekend (ويكاند) ou pour événements et groupes (groupe, مناسبات).

Localisations mentionnées
- Palmeraie Marrakech : cadre verdoyant et calme, souvent demandé pour villas familiales.
- Route de l’Ourika (Ourika) : proximité de la vallée de l’Ourika pour excursions nature.
- Route de Fès, Targa et proximité aéroport : options selon emplacement précis de la villa.

Conseils de réservation
- Vérifier la disponibilité pour les séjours courts (journée, weekend) et les prestations inclues (chef, ménage).
- Confirmer l’emplacement exact (Palmeraie, Ourika, Targa ou proche aéroport) pour planifier transferts et excursions.
- Demander photos récentes de la piscine et des chambres avant paiement.

Pour qui est cette villa
Idéale pour familles nombreuses, groupes d’amis ou événements privés cherchant une grande villa avec piscine à proximité de Marrakech, avec possibilités de services sur demande.

Conclusion / Appel à l’action
Contacter l’annonceur pour tarifs, disponibilité et services personnalisés (chef, ménage) et préciser la localisation souhaitée (Palmeraie, Ourika ou proche aéroport) pour finaliser la réservation.

Book a 15–30 minute sunset paragliding flight over the Atlas and Agafay Desert from Marrakech. Free hotel transfer; filmed with GoPro. Price 70€ (700 MAD). #visitmarrakech #agafaydesert #marrakechactivities
Experience sunset paragliding in Marrakech: short flights over the Atlas and Agafay Desert

Overview
Fly 15 to 30 minutes above the Atlas Mountains and the lunar plains of the Agafay Desert on a sunset paragliding flight departing 45 minutes from Marrakech. The service includes free transfer from your hotel and flights are recorded with a GoPro mounted on a camera stick. Price: 70€ (700 MAD).

What to expect
- Duration: 15–30 minutes of airborne time, ideal for a sunset experience. 
- Location: Launch site about 45 minutes from Marrakech with views of the Atlas Mountains and the Agafay Desert. 
- Transfer: Complimentary hotel pickup and drop-off from Marrakech to the launch site. 
- Recording: Flights filmed with a GoPro camera stick so you can keep a visual memory of the experience.
- Price: 70€ (700 MAD) per person.

Why choose this flight
This option is tailored for travelers seeking a concise, scenic aerial experience near Marrakech. The timing around sunset highlights the desert colors and mountain silhouettes, while the free transfer removes logistics stress. description:
Hidden gems in Marrakech - trust me, you won’t regret it;) 📍Souk Serajine (next to Madrasa Ben Youssef) - for its craftsmanship and authenticity (especially if you’re looking for leather goods)  📍Souk Talaa (also in the Madrasa Ben Youssef neighborhood) - the little place in the middle is beautiful and surrounded by so many cute shops, far away from the tourist traps 📍Deserted Photo Booth - we discovered it late at night after dinner and had so much fun; the perfect little memory to take home  📍Daraavalleytravel - if you’re looking for pottery, this is your spot ! Everything is handmade by the owner himself (funny guy) and soooo pretty, almost bought the whole shop 📍Route Sidi Abdelaziz - this is where you can find Bacha Coffee, and you have to go ! We went there on our way to the WA SPA and discovered such a lovely neighborhood 📍Amasmoud art - beautiful place if you’re looking for a rug, the owner is so sweet and has so many high quality pieces 
Discover Marrakech's best kept secrets! Explore authentic souks like Souk Serajine and Souk Talaa, find unique pottery at Daraavalleytravel, and enjoy Bacha Coffee. Your unforgettable Marrakech adventure starts here!
Looking for the real Marrakech beyond the usual tourist trails? This guide uncovers hidden gems that promise authenticity and unforgettable experiences. Prepare to be delighted!

**Souk Serajine:** Nestled next to the Madrasa Ben Youssef, this souk is a treasure trove of craftsmanship. It's particularly noteworthy for its authentic leather goods, offering a genuine glimpse into traditional Moroccan artistry.

**Souk Talaa:** Also located in the vibrant Madrasa Ben Youssef neighborhood, Souk Talaa offers a charming escape. The central area is exceptionally beautiful, surrounded by delightful shops that steer clear of typical tourist crowds.

**Deserted Photo Booth:** A delightful discovery made late one night after dinner, this quirky photo booth provides a fun and unique way to capture memories from your trip. It's the perfect, lighthearted memento.

**Daraavalleytravel:** For those seeking exquisite handmade pottery, this is the ultimate destination. The owner, a character in himself, crafts stunning pieces that are almost too beautiful to resist – you might find yourself wanting to buy the entire collection!

**Route Sidi Abdelaziz:** This route leads to the iconic Bacha Coffee, a must-visit spot. While en route to the WA SPA, a charming neighborhood unfolded, revealing this delightful destination.

**Amasmoud Art:** If you're in search of a beautiful rug, Amasmoud Art is your go-to. The owner's sweet demeanor complements the collection of high-quality rugs, showcasing remarkable artistry.

Explore these special places and make your Marrakech journey truly unique. You won't regret discovering these authentic corners of the city!

description:
Save for your 3 day itinerary in Marrakech 🇲🇦  Some tips when visiting Marrakech: ✨ Pre-book restaurants especially if in bigger groups or on a short trip. ✨ Barter in Souks when making purchases.  ✨ Wear comfortable footwear when visiting the Souks as you will be walking around for some time. ✨ Don’t take photos of animals without permission or you may be charged. ✨ Purchase a SIM card at the airport so you have internet when WiFi isn’t available  ✨ Use google maps to navigate your way around the Souks. ✨ Convert money into Dirhams at the airport. Most restaurants will take card but cash is better when visiting the Souks or giving tips. ATMs are available too. ✨ When taking taxi, ensure the metre is set before your journey or agree a fee before hand. We hired a private taxi for the duration of our stay which made travelling very easy. ✨ Dress more conservatively in the day, we wore linen pants or long casual dresses whilst evening attire in restaurants was more relaxed.  ✨ Don’t drink tap water. ✨ Don’t accept directions/tours from people you don’t know. ✨ Visit a rooftop restaurant during the day or at sunset for scenic views. #morocco #marrakechguide #marrakech #marrakech #marrakechfood  @Dardar Rooftop Marrakech  @LA TRATTORIA MARRAKECH  @Buddha Bar Marrakech  @Safran Marrakech  @Marajah Spa 
Explore Marrakech with our essential 3-day itinerary tips! Perfect for navigating the Souks and enjoying the best experiences. #morocco #marrakechguide #marrakech
Marrakech is a vibrant city full of rich cultural heritage, bustling markets, and stunning architecture. Planning a 3-day itinerary can be overwhelming, but with a few essential tips, you can make the most of your visit to this enchanting destination. Here are some helpful strategies to navigate your time in Marrakech effectively.

### Pre-Planning Your Visit
One of the first tips for visiting Marrakech is to pre-book your restaurants, especially if you're traveling in larger groups or only have a short trip. This ensures you won’t miss out on popular dining spots and can enjoy the local cuisine stress-free.

### Shopping in the Souks
When exploring the famous Souks, make sure to practice your bartering skills. It's a customary practice in local markets, and you may find much better prices if you negotiate. Also, wear comfortable footwear as you'll likely be walking around for an extended period.

### Respecting Local Customs
While in Marrakech, there are a few customs to be aware of. Avoid taking photos of animals without permission, as some may charge you for it. It’s also advisable to dress conservatively during the day; consider wearing linen pants or long casual dresses to respect the local culture. Evening attires at restaurants tend to be more relaxed, allowing for a bit more comfort.

### Connectivity and Navigation
Purchasing a SIM card at the airport can be beneficial for internet access when WiFi isn’t available. Use Google Maps to navigate the intricate paths of the Souks, making sure you have a good sense of direction.

### Currency and Payments
Always convert some money into Dirhams upon arrival at the airport. While most restaurants accept cards, cash is generally preferred in the Souks, especially for tips. There are also ATMs available should you need to withdraw more cash.

### Safe Travel Tips
For an easier travel experience, particularly when using taxis, ensure that the meter is set before your journey or agree on a fee beforehand. Hiring a private taxi for your stay can simplify your transportation needs.

### Hydration and Safety
Do not drink tap water during your stay; opting for bottled water is a safer choice. Lastly, be cautious about accepting directions or tours from strangers, and instead rely on trusted sources or your own navigation tools.

### Scenic Dining Experiences
Finally, don't miss the chance to visit a rooftop restaurant, especially during the day or at sunset, to take in the breathtaking views of the city. The combination of stunning landscapes and delicious cuisine can provide a memorable dining experience.

With these tips in hand, your 3-day adventure in Marrakech will be both enjoyable and enriching. Embrace the unique culture and hospitality that Marrakech has to offer, and make the most out of your visit!

description:
I’m still here cuz what a perfect day to just stroll through the beautiful Medina  #thingstodoinmarrakech #marrakechitinerary #marrakech #travel #morocco 
Discover a perfect day in Marrakech with our recommended itinerary featuring must-see spots and delicious meals. #thingstodoinmarrakech #morocco
Marrakech is a city that captures the essence of Moroccan culture and history, making it a perfect destination for a day of exploration. Begin your journey with a delightful breakfast at **Olema**, where you can enjoy the flavors of traditional Moroccan cuisine surrounded by beautiful decor. After your meal, head to **Le Jardin Secret**, a peaceful escape that showcases the artistry of Moroccan garden design and architecture.

Once you've soaked in the tranquility, dive into the vibrant **Medina**, the heart of Marrakech. Here, you'll find bustling markets filled with spices, textiles, and handcrafted goods. A minor but delightful experience is to get **henna** done by a local artist at the market, an age-old tradition reflecting the Moroccan culture.

For lunch, enjoy a meal at **Le Slimana**, known for its delicious Moroccan dishes, where you can savor fresh ingredients and rich flavors. After recharging, take a leisurely **walk through the market**, exploring the various stalls and soaking in the lively atmosphere.

No visit to Marrakech is complete without exploring the stunning **Madrasa Ben Youssef**, an ancient Islamic school known for its intricate Moroccan architecture. This site offers a glimpse into the educational practices of the past and the beauty of Moroccan design.

To wrap up your day, consider taking a refreshing swim before dressing up for a memorable dinner and show at **Comptoir Darna**. Here, you’ll enjoy a blend of exquisite Moroccan cuisine and captivating entertainment with traditional music and dance.

This itinerary promises not just sightseeing but a day filled with unique experiences that reveal the charm of Marrakech. For an unforgettable travel experience, don't forget to immerse yourself in the city’s vibrant culture and rich history.

description:
Marrakesh in itself has so much to see and explore but these are some of my favourite day trips we did from Marrakesh.  1. Ouzud Falls  These falls are breathtaking and are in fact the second tallest waterfall in Africa. The hike isnt too strenuous. The views are definitely worth it. The tour bus we took stopped off at these argan trees where we saw the goats. Opportunity to buy some pure argan oil here too.  Approx 2hr 30mins from Marrakesh 2 Essaouira  This is a beautiful seaside town with some of the best fresh fish i have ever tasted. Caught moments before cooking. Really nice streets where you can buy the most authentic Moroccan goods.  Approx 3hrs from Marrakesh  I would definitely recommend both trips. They cost roughly £20-£30pp for return transfer from Marrakesh.  One trip i will do when i return is to tge Agafay dessert.  Meant to be amazing.  #marrakech #morocco #marrakechdaytrip #agafaydesert #essaouira #ouzud #travelmorocco 
Uncover the beauty of Marrakesh with day trips to Ouzud Falls and Essaouira. Experience breathtaking nature and fresh seafood! #marrakech #morocco #travelmorocco

description:
Day 3 in Marrakech | Morocco Vlog🇲🇦 #fyp #fashiontiktok #morocco #marrakech 
Join us as we discover the vibrant culture and aesthetics of Marrakech on Day 3 of our Morocco vlog! #morocco #marrakech
Marrakech, the vibrant heart of Morocco, offers an array of experiences that blend culture, cuisine, and captivating aesthetics. On Day 3 of our Morocco vlog, we delve deeper into this enchanting city, showcasing its unique charm.  
  
As we explore the streets of Marrakech, the vibrant colors and bustling markets catch our attention, particularly the clothing options that reflect the rich culture of Morocco. We highlight various Moroccan outfits that embody the spirit and style of the region. From traditional garments to modern adaptations, each piece tells a story of its heritage.  
  
Food is an integral part of any journey, and Marrakech doesn't disappoint. We sample local dishes that tantalize our taste buds and provide insight into the culinary traditions of the area. From flavorful tagines to sweet pastries, each bite is a celebration of Moroccan cuisine.  
  
Shopping is a must in Marrakech, with its myriad of shops and stalls. We explore local marketplaces, providing tips on finding authentic Moroccan crafts and souvenirs. The aesthetics of these markets, filled with artisan pieces, create a memorable shopping experience.  
  
Throughout our adventures, we showcase the unique aesthetic of Marrakech, from its stunning architecture to the mesmerizing street art. Each corner of the city serves as a backdrop to our journey, making it unforgettable.  
  
As we wrap up Day 3 in Marrakech, we reflect on our favorite moments and encourage our viewers to discover the beauty and warmth of Morocco. Whether it's the allure of the food, the charm of the clothing, or the vibrancy of the city, Marrakech leaves a lasting impression on all who visit.

description:
Spend day 1 & 2 in marrakech with us ✨  • we stayed at riad BE which was beautiful & staff were amazing • had the best rooftop lunch at dardar  • romantic dinner at PSC  • bacha coffee museum is a must • yummy rooftop lunch at otto  • simple for iced lattes - make sure you try the moroccan spiced syrup  • the best chocolate mousse ever ever at mizaan  #marrakechtravel #marrakechbestplaces #vlog 
Explore Marrakech with us! Day 1 & 2 highlights include Riad BE, rooftop dining at DarDdar & Otto, a must-visit Bacha Coffee Museum, and romantic dinner at PSC.
Our first two days in Marrakech offered a delightful immersion into the city's charm. We chose to stay at Riad BE, a truly beautiful riad with incredibly amazing staff who made our stay memorable.

For culinary experiences, we enjoyed a fantastic rooftop lunch at DarDdar, a romantic dinner at PSC, and another delicious rooftop lunch at Otto. These spots provided not only great food but also stunning views.

A visit to the Bacha Coffee Museum is an absolute must for any traveler in Marrakech. It's an experience that combines history, culture, and of course, coffee.

We also discovered Simple, a charming spot for iced lattes. Don't miss trying their Moroccan spiced syrup – it adds a unique and delightful twist to your drink.

And for dessert lovers, Mizaán served up what we can only describe as the best chocolate mousse ever. It was a perfect sweet ending to our explorations.

#marrakechtravel #marrakechbestplaces #vlog

description:
one of the prettiest villages in the mountains of Morocco #chefchaouen #morocco #placestovisit #moroccotravel #bluepearl 
Discover Chefchaouen, the blue pearl in Morocco's mountains: top sights, best photo spots, and travel tips for visiting this picturesque village. #chefchaouen #morocco #placestovisit #moroccotravel #bluepearl
Lead
Chefchaouen — often called the Blue Pearl — is one of the prettiest mountain villages in Morocco. Nestled in the Rif Mountains, its lanes, bluewashed buildings, and scenic viewpoints make it a top destination for photographers and travelers seeking a compact, colorful Moroccan experience.

What makes Chefchaouen special
- Distinctive blue-painted medina streets that create striking photo opportunities. 
- Mountain setting offering nearby hikes, panoramic viewpoints, and cooler air than coastal cities.
- A compact, walkable old town with artisanal shops, local cafes, and traditional Moroccan architecture.

Top sights and photo spots
- Medina alleys: wander narrow lanes lined with blue walls, doors, and stairways at every turn. Perfect for morning or late-afternoon light.
- Plaza Uta el-Hammam: the central square for people-watching, dining, and gateway views into the medina.
- Kasbah and gardens: a historic fortress with a small garden and museum; climb nearby streets for elevated views.
- Outskirts viewpoints: short hikes from the town reveal sweeping views of the blue roofs against mountain backdrops.

Practical travel tips
- Best time to visit: spring and autumn for mild weather; summer can be busy but still popular. Early morning avoids crowds and captures softer light for photos. 
- Getting there: Chefchaouen is commonly reached by road from Tangier, Fez, or Casablanca. Buses, shared taxis, and private transfers are typical options. 
- Accommodation: a range of guesthouses and riads inside and around the medina — choose one near the center to maximize walking access. 
- Respect local customs: modest dress helps in conservative areas; ask before photographing people. 
- Shopping: look for woven blankets, pottery, leather goods, and local crafts in the medina. Haggling is common but polite bargaining yields fair prices.

Nearby activities
- Day hikes in the Rif Mountains for nature and panoramic views. 
- Short excursions to nearby waterfalls, rural villages, or coastal cities if you have extra time.

Closing / Call to action
Chefchaouen’s blue lanes and mountain setting offer a memorable, photogenic stop on any Morocco itinerary. Plan for a relaxed pace, carry a camera for the iconic blue scenes, and leave time to wander; the village’s charm reveals itself in its streets and views. Have you visited Chefchaouen or planning a trip? Consider which photo spots you most want to see and when you’ll go to avoid the crowds.

description:
Little Marrakech series - day 1 🇲🇦 #dayinmylifevlog #travelblogger 
Join us for Day 1 in Marrakech! From a stunning riad with a resident tortoise to exploring vibrant markets and enjoying rooftop drinks, see all our adventures. Follow for Day 2! #dayinmylifevlog #travelblogger
Our Marrakech adventure begins with Day 1 in the vibrant city. Upon landing, our initial accommodation unexpectedly cancelled, but this turned out to be a fortunate turn of events. We discovered an incredible alternative riad, complete with a resident tortoise, perfectly situated on the outskirts of the souk.

For lunch, we visited a visually stunning spot, though the food itself didn't quite meet expectations. Afterward, we immersed ourselves in the bustling markets, searching for treasures. As evening approached, we headed for pre-dinner drinks at Nobu Rooftop. While the aesthetic was pleasing, the grey skies meant there wasn't much of a sunset to enjoy, and the food menu felt somewhat limited.

Dinner was at Saffron, a place we highly recommend, particularly for their cocktails. It was a nice and easy first night, setting the tone for our explorations. Follow along for Day 2 of our Marrakech journey.

description:
Marrakech can be for families too! These are some things we did whilst we were there 🇲🇦 We just had the best week here with 2 children! One with a disability and still got to experience so much!!  Some exclaimers: • Staying in a resort means having to get a taxi to places as its about 30 minutes away from most things. • Go during the UK winter months as its cooler (between 21 - 28 degrees) in the later months it gets too hot (over 40 degrees!)  • For the camel riding, go in the AM as it gets too hot after midday. • Be aware of people trying to scam you in the centre (always stay close and say no as they will expect money from you. • Have fun! Theres so many lovely people you meet along the way. #downsyndrome #marrakesh #familyholiday #thingstodoinmarrakesh #fyp

Explore the best activities in Marrakech with this 4-day itinerary! Discover must-visit sites and delicious food options. #marrakesh #marrakechguide #morocco
Marrakech, a city rich in culture and vibrant experiences, offers an array of activities perfect for a four-day adventure. This itinerary encapsulates the essence of Marrakech, ensuring visitors get an authentic taste of what the city has to offer.

**Day 1: Iconic Landmarks**  
Start your journey by visiting the Koutoubia Mosque, an iconic symbol of Marrakech that stands tall with its stunning architecture. Following this, embark on a sunrise hot air balloon ride, providing breathtaking views of the city and surrounding Atlas Mountains. Afterward, pamper yourself with a traditional hammam spa experience—an essential part of Moroccan culture.

**Day 2: Cultural Exploration**  
Delve into Marrakech’s history by visiting the Bahia Palace and the El Badi Palace. These exquisite sites reflect the grandeur of Moroccan architecture. To further immerse yourself in local culture, consider booking a cooking class where you can learn how to prepare traditional Moroccan dishes.

**Day 3: Natural Wonders and Relaxation**  
Take a trip to the Atlas Mountains and visit Berber villages, experiencing the stunning landscapes and local traditions. Don’t miss the Ouzoud Waterfalls, where you can enjoy nature at its finest. After a day of exploration, take a leisurely stroll through the secret gardens and vibrant squares of the Medina.

**Day 4: Unique Experiences and Culinary Delights**  
For a unique dining experience, reserve a table at Dada Rooftop for drinks or be among the foodie crowds at Nobu for a fancier vibe. If you enjoy jazz, make sure to visit libistro paste for incredible food. However, be cautious about Comforto Donna due to mixed reviews regarding food quality, as some travelers found it lacking flavor.  
 
Wrap up your trip with a visit to the Agafay desert for camel riding or quad biking. Remember to book these experiences through GetYourGuide.com using the discount code ‘TASTETRAVELSOCIAL5’ for savings!  
 
Overall, Marrakech is a city that blends tradition with modernity, offering something for every kind of traveler. With this itinerary, you’ll be able to experience the true essence of Marrakech while enjoying its culinary delights and breathtaking landscapes. Be sure to save and follow for more Marrakech recommendations on your next trip!

description:
Je comprends enfin pourquoi le Jardin Majorelle est l’un des lieux les plus visités de Marrakech 💙🌵 Entre ses cactus venus des quatre coins du monde, ses plantes exotiques, ses bassins paisibles et ce bleu Majorelle devenu emblématique, l’endroit est aussi beau qu’apaisant 😍 Une véritable parenthèse de calme au cœur de Marrakech, qui mérite largement sa réputation ! #voyage #maroc #marrakech #jardinmajorelle #travel 
Découvrez pourquoi le Jardin Majorelle à Marrakech séduit : cactus du monde, plantes exotiques, bassins paisibles et le bleu Majorelle emblématique. #voyage #maroc #marrakech #jardinmajorelle #travel
Le Jardin Majorelle à Marrakech est l’un des lieux les plus visités de la ville — et pour de bonnes raisons. Entre collections botaniques, architecture colorée et atmosphère apaisante, il offre une parenthèse de calme au cœur de l’effervescence marrakchie.

Un bleu devenu emblème
Le bleu Majorelle, omniprésent dans le jardin, attire immédiatement le regard. Cette teinte vive, utilisée sur murs et structures, crée un contraste puissant avec le vert des plantes et le sable environnant. Le bleu est aujourd’hui associé au lieu et contribue fortement à son identité visuelle.

Collections de plantes et cactus du monde
Le jardin rassemble des cactus venus des quatre coins du monde et des plantes exotiques soigneusement disposées. Chaque allée révèle une variété botanique différente — succulentes, palmiers et espèces rares — qui séduisent autant les amateurs de botanique que les visiteurs recherchant des décors photogéniques.

Bassins et atmosphère apaisante
Des bassins paisibles ponctuent le jardin, apportant fraîcheur et tranquillité. Le murmure de l’eau et l’ombrage des plantes créent une ambiance reposante, idéale pour se détendre loin du tumulte de la médina.

Pourquoi visiter ?
- Esthétique unique : le mariage du bleu Majorelle et de la végétation crée des compositions visuelles exceptionnelles.
- Diversité botanique : cactus et plantes exotiques offrent une visite instructive et sensorielle.
- Calme en ville : un espace de repos et de contemplation au cœur de Marrakech.
- Photogénique : angles, couleurs et bassins en font un lieu très prisé pour la photographie.

Conseils pratiques
- Prévoir une visite en dehors des heures de pointe pour profiter pleinement du calme.
- Apporter une bouteille d’eau et un chapeau : certaines allées sont dégagées et ensoleillées.
- Respecter les espaces plantés et suivre les indications sur place afin de préserver la collection.

En résumé
Le Jardin Majorelle mérite sa réputation : il est à la fois beau et apaisant, grâce à ses cactus internationaux, ses plantes exotiques, ses bassins tranquilles et le fameux bleu Majorelle. Une visite recommandée pour quiconque cherche une pause sereine et photogénique à Marrakech.

description_clean: je comprends enfin pourquoi le jardin majorelle est l’un des lieux les plus visités de marrakech 💙🌵 entre ses cactus venus des quatre coins du monde, ses plantes exotiques, ses bassins paisibles et ce bleu majorelle devenu emblématique, l’endroit est aussi beau qu’apaisant 😍 une véritable parenthèse de calme au cœur de marrakech, qui mérite largement sa réputation ! voyage maroc marrakech jardinmajorelle travel  découvrez pourquoi le jardin majorelle à marrakech séduit : cactus du monde, plantes exotiques, bassins paisibles et le bleu majorelle emblématique. voyage maroc marrakech jardinmajorelle travel le jardin majorelle à marrakech est l’un des lieux les plus visités de la ville — et pour de bonnes raisons. entre collections botaniques, architecture colorée et atmosphère apaisante, il offre une parenthèse de calme au cœur de l’effervescence marrakchie.  un bleu devenu emblème le bleu majorelle, omniprésent dans le jardin, attire immédiatement le regard. cette teinte vive, utilisée sur murs et structures, crée un contraste puissant avec le vert des plantes et le sable environnant. le bleu est aujourd’hui associé au lieu et contribue fortement à son identité visuelle.  collections de plantes et cactus du monde le jardin rassemble des cactus venus des quatre coins du monde et des plantes exotiques soigneusement disposées. chaque allée révèle une variété botanique différente — succulentes, palmiers et espèces rares — qui séduisent autant les amateurs de botanique que les visiteurs recherchant des décors photogéniques.  bassins et atmosphère apaisante des bassins paisibles ponctuent le jardin, apportant fraîcheur et tranquillité. le murmure de l’eau et l’ombrage des plantes créent une ambiance reposante, idéale pour se détendre loin du tumulte de la médina.  pourquoi visiter ? - esthétique unique : le mariage du bleu majorelle et de la végétation crée des compositions visuelles exceptionnelles. - diversité botanique : cactus et plantes exotiques offrent une visite instructive et sensorielle. - calme en ville : un espace de repos et de contemplation au cœur de marrakech. - photogénique : angles, couleurs et bassins en font un lieu très prisé pour la photographie.  conseils pratiques - prévoir une visite en dehors des heures de pointe pour profiter pleinement du calme. - apporter une bouteille d’eau et un chapeau : certaines allées sont dégagées et ensoleillées. - respecter les espaces plantés et suivre les indications sur place afin de préserver la collection.  en résumé le jardin majorelle mérite sa réputation : il est à la fois beau et apaisant, grâce à ses cactus internationaux, ses plantes exotiques, ses bassins tranquilles et le fameux bleu majorelle. une visite recommandée pour quiconque cherche une pause sereine et photogénique à marrakech.  avez-vous déjà visité le jardin majorelle ? description:
This is one of the best trips of my life #okavangodelta #botswana #solotravel #grwm #safari 
Firsthand solo safari in the Okavango Delta — highlights, tips, and what made this Botswana trip unforgettable. #okavangodelta #botswana #solotravel #grwm #safari
Lead
A solo safari in the Okavango Delta, Botswana, can be one of the most memorable trips of your life. This account focuses on a personal, first‑person solo travel experience: getting ready (GRWM), key safari highlights, practical tips for solo travelers, and what made the Okavango Delta stand out.

What to expect on a Botswana solo safari
- Landscape and wildlife: Wide waterways, delta channels, and rich wildlife sightings characterize the Okavango Delta. Expect mokoro (dugout canoe) rides, game drives, and close encounters with elephants, buffalo, antelope, and birds.
- Pace and atmosphere: The Delta’s pace is relaxed; days are driven by light, animal activity, and seasonal water levels.

Preparing for a solo trip (GRWM essentials)
- Packing: Lightweight neutral clothing for game drives, sun protection (hat, SPF), sturdy shoes, insect repellent, binoculars, and a good camera. Bring a compact first‑aid kit and any personal medications.
- Documentation: Confirm visas, travel insurance that covers safari activities, and vaccination requirements. Keep copies of important documents both physical and digital.
- Communication: Local SIM or satellite options depending on camp remoteness. Share your itinerary with someone at home.

Safety and solo traveler tips
- Choose reputable camps or lodges with strong guest reviews and clear safety protocols.
- Join guided activities: Most wildlife sightings and safe experiences come with experienced guides — use them, especially at dawn and dusk.
- Respect wildlife and camp rules: Keep distance, follow guides’ instructions, and avoid solo walks after dark.

Highlights that make the trip unforgettable
- Close wildlife encounters led by local guides who read animal behavior.
- Unique Delta experiences such as mokoro trips and walking safaris that bring a different perspective from vehicle drives.
- Quiet evenings at camp under clear skies — a chance to reflect on the day’s sightings and the Delta’s ecosystem.

Practical itinerary ideas for a short solo safari
- Day 1: Arrival in Maun, orientation, and overnight near town.
- Day 2–4: Transfer into the Okavango Delta, mix of mokoro, game drives, and walking safaris with a lodge or mobile camp.
- Day 5: Return to Maun and depart or extend to Chobe or Moremi for more wildlife viewing.

Photography and sharing the experience
- Bring a zoom lens for wildlife, a compact gimbal for video, and extra batteries and memory cards. Capture the GRWM moments briefly: packing, pre‑travel thoughts, and brief camp scenes to tell the story of solo travel.

Closing takeaway
A solo safari in Botswana’s Okavango Delta combines solitude, expert-guided wildlife viewing, and immersive nature experiences. Plan thoughtfully, choose trusted operators, pack smart, and let the Delta’s rhythms create one of your best trips. Interested in planning your own solo safari? Save these tips and start with reputable lodges and guided activities to get the most from the Okavango Delta.

Hashtags
#okavangodelta #botswana #solotravel #grwm #safari

description: If you think Marrakech is only for the young, you are mistaken. It’s a city that offers so much for all ages.  . We took our teen with us and she had an amazing time. She’s at an age where “ I’m bored “ is her fave word, but glad she didn’t say that even once in Morocco.  . On our first day we covered: 🕌 BEN YOUSSEF MEDERSA- the tickets were €4 and they accept cash only. We did get an audio guide and honestly it wasn’t much helpful. It just read what was written on the placards by the walls . 🕌 SOUKS - carry cash if you are planning to shop. We picked out spices, coasters and some Knick-knacks for the house.  🕌 YSL MUSEUM - we missed the memo and didn’t buy tickets to the museum amd marjorelle gardens ahead of time and they were sold out for the whole week. We were able to get tickets for the museum but it wasn’t enough for us. Be proactive and get your tickets as soon as you have your itinerary locked in.  🕌 @lejardinmarrakech a garden restaurant worth trying out. Their cocktails and fish tajine was delicious.  🕌RIAD - I can’t emphasize enough on the experience  you get by staying in a RIAD when in Morocco. It’s not the typical hotel but a house turned into a hotel with a very homely comfortable stay.  : #thingstodoinmarrakech #moroccowithkids

description:
The best affordable riad in Marrakesh 💜 this riad is located in the heart of the Medina and walking distance from the souks. The service was incredible and so was the breakfast! I would gladly stay here again. Everyone is so welcoming and they even offer hamman services. Highly recommend staying here if you are staying in Marrakesh #marrakech #morocco #riad #moroccohotels #marrakech🇲🇦morocco  
Discover the best riad in Marrakesh, offering amazing service and breakfast, just steps from the souks. Highly recommended! #marrakech #morocco #riad #moroccohotels
Marrakesh, a vibrant city known for its rich culture and stunning architecture, offers travelers numerous accommodations, but few are as delightful as the best affordable riad located in the heart of the Medina. This riad is not only within walking distance of the famous souks, but it also provides guests with exceptional service and an inviting atmosphere that makes them feel right at home.

Upon entering the riad, visitors are greeted with warm hospitality, and the stunning decor captivates the senses. From intricately designed tiles to lush indoor gardens, the ambiance is both tranquil and exotic. Guests rave about the incredible service, which makes their stay even more enjoyable.

One of the highlights of this riad is the breakfast. Every morning, guests can indulge in delicious, traditional Moroccan dishes that offer a taste of local flavors. Whether you prefer fresh pastries, fruit, or hearty local dishes, the breakfast spread does not disappoint, setting the tone for a day of exploration in Marrakesh.

Another unique feature of this riad is the availability of hamman services, allowing guests to relax and rejuvenate in a traditional Moroccan bath experience. This addition to a stay makes it even more memorable.

Staying at this riad is not just about having a place to rest; it’s about enjoying a complete cultural experience. If you are planning a trip to Marrakesh, this riad comes highly recommended by past guests. Don’t miss out on the charm and hospitality it has to offer. A stay here means you will return from your trip feeling refreshed and enriched by the beauty of Morocco.

Explore the lively markets of Marrakech, Morocco. Perfect for your next travel adventure! #marrakech #morocco #bucketlist #wanderlust #markets
Marrakech, Morocco, is a city renowned for its vibrant and bustling markets that offer a unique glimpse into the local culture and lifestyle. From colorful textiles to traditional handicrafts, the markets here are packed with life and energy. As you navigate the winding alleys, the sounds of merchants calling out and the scent of spices wafting through the air create an immersive experience unlike any other. 

When visiting these markets, make sure to explore the famous souks, where you can find everything from handcrafted pottery to authentic Moroccan lanterns. Haggling is part of the shopping experience, so be prepared for some lively negotiations! These markets not only provide an opportunity to shop but also to engage with the local community and learn more about their traditions and crafts. 

Whether you're seeking unique souvenirs or simply looking to soak in the vibrant atmosphere, the markets of Marrakech are a must-visit on your travel itinerary. Be sure to save this guide for your next trip and follow for more travel spots around the world!

description:
Found the bag I wanted!  #marrakech #morocco #travelvlog #shopping #travel 
Join me as I explore Marrakech and find the perfect bag! Discover the best shopping spots and travel tips. #marrakech #morocco #travelvlog #shopping #travel
Marrakech, the vibrant heart of Morocco, is known for its rich culture, stunning architecture, and bustling markets. Recently, I embarked on an exciting shopping adventure across this magnificent city, and I'm thrilled to share my experience of finding the perfect bag!  

Wandering through the lively streets, I was captivated by the colorful stalls overflowing with handmade goods and unique treasures. The markets in Marrakech are not just places to shop; they are a cultural experience, where you can immerse yourself in the local traditions and craftsmanship. From leather to textiles, each stall tells a story through its products.  

One of the highlights of my trip was visiting the famous souks, where I discovered various bags, each more beautiful than the last. The artisans' skill and attention to detail were evident in every piece. I wanted something distinctive to take back as a memento from my travels, and I certainly found it! The bag I ultimately chose featured traditional Moroccan designs, showcasing the vibrant colors and intricate patterns the region is renowned for.

Shopping in Marrakech is not just about purchasing items; it's about interacting with the locals, bargaining for the best price, and appreciating the art of craftsmanship. If you're planning a trip to Morocco, make sure to include ample time in your itinerary for shopping in Marrakech's souks.  

To all travel enthusiasts and shoppers alike: what unique items have you found during your travels? Share your experiences and tips in the comments below! This city is a treasure trove waiting to be explored, and I highly recommend dive into the shopping scene—you never know what you might find!

description:
The best activities to do in Marrakesh Morocco 🇲🇦 ##camelriding##thingstodoinmarrakesh##fyp##traveltok##quadbiking
Discover top activities in Marrakesh: camel riding, quad biking, and must-visit spots to plan your Morocco trip. #camelriding #thingstodoinmarrakesh #traveltok
Highlights of Activities in Marrakesh, Morocco

Marrakesh offers a mix of desert adventure and city discovery. Top activities include camel riding, quad biking, and exploring iconic places across the city and nearby desert. These experiences suit first-time visitors and repeat travelers seeking both culture and outdoor thrills.

Camel Riding

Camel riding is a signature Marrakesh activity. Tours range from short rides at sunset to longer excursions into nearby dunes and palm groves. Expect traditional guides, simple saddles, and photogenic landscapes ideal for capturing memories.

Quad Biking

Quad biking around Marrakesh provides an adrenaline-filled way to see semi-desert terrain and palm-filled outskirts. Guided quad tours usually include safety briefings, protective gear, and routes that combine open tracks with scenic stops.

City Sights and Places to Visit

Marrakesh city experiences complement outdoor adventures. Popular places to visit include historic squares, markets, and gardens. Wander the medina, sample local food, and visit notable landmarks to balance active outings with cultural immersion.

Planning Tips

- Book activities through reputable operators that provide safety equipment and clear instructions.
- Time outdoor excursions for morning or late afternoon to avoid peak heat.
- Combine a desert activity (camel ride or quad biking) with city visits for a full Marrakesh itinerary.

Takeaway

Marrakesh blends adventure and culture: camel riding and quad biking deliver memorable outdoor experiences, while the city’s markets and landmarks complete a well-rounded Morocco visit. Which activity will you try first—camel riding at sunset or a quad biking tour?

description:
Don’t think I’ll ever process this moment 🥹✨ #marrakech #morocco #hotairballoonride 
Experience an unforgettable hot air balloon ride over Marrakech, Morocco. Fulfilling a true bucket list moment. ✨ #marrakech #morocco #hotairballoonride
Witnessing a sunrise from a hot air balloon over Marrakech is a moment that truly stays with you. This breathtaking experience is a quintessential bucket list item for many, and for good reason. Floating gently above the landscape as the first light paints the sky offers a unique and serene perspective of the Moroccan terrain.

The adventure begins before dawn, building anticipation for the ascent. As the balloon inflates and lifts off, the world below transforms into a miniature tableau. The vastness of the desert and the intricate patterns of the city unfold, creating a scene of unparalleled beauty. This is more than just a ride; it's an immersive journey that provides a sense of peace and wonder.

Many describe this as a core memory, a profound experience that resonates long after the flight has ended. For those seeking an extraordinary travel highlight in Morocco, a hot air balloon ride over Marrakech delivers an unparalleled sense of awe and accomplishment.

description: Most tourists plan Morocco by pinning famous places on the map… but Morocco feels very different once the trip actually starts. Distances are bigger than people expect, Atlas mountain roads slow movement down, and hotel switching across the country can become exhausting fast. Instead of trying to rush Marrakech, Chefchaouen, the Sahara and everything else in one trip, Morocco usually feels much better when you travel by zones and slow the pace down. Marrakech + Atlas Mountains + Essaouira. Fes + Chefchaouen. Or a dedicated Southern Morocco + Sahara route. The trip becomes far more enjoyable once the movement starts making sense geographically. #morocco #moroccotravel #marrakech #saharadesert #travelplanning

Riad Yasmine is truly a hidden oasis nestled in the heart of Marrakech, Morocco. Renowned for its stunning architecture and serene ambiance, this luxurious riad offers an unparalleled experience for travelers seeking a unique getaway.

The beauty of Riad Yasmine lies not just in its picturesque surroundings but also in the intricate details of its design. Visitors can immerse themselves in rich Moroccan tradition while enjoying modern comforts. The riad features lush gardens, tranquil courtyards, and beautifully designed rooms that invite relaxation and reflection. 

Marrakech itself is a city that pulses with life and culture. With vibrant souks and historical landmarks just steps away, Riad Yasmine serves as the perfect base for exploring the wonders of this enchanting city. 

Whether you’re lounging by the pool or sipping mint tea in the courtyard, Riad Yasmine captures the essence of Moroccan hospitality. It’s more than just a place to stay; it’s an experience that resonates with the spirit of Marrakech.

For those craving luxury and authenticity, this hidden gem is a must-visit destination. Plan your escape to Riad Yasmine and indulge in the beauty and charm of Morocco today!

Marrakech is a city known for its vibrant culture and delectable cuisine, making it a top destination for food enthusiasts. When visiting this beautiful city, a meal at the acclaimed 10/10 Diner is an experience not to be missed. Located in the heart of Marrakech, this diner offers a unique dining atmosphere that brings traditional Moroccan flavors to life.

description:
Before there was @Jnane Tamsna Boutique Hotel there was Dar Tamsna, the first boutique hotel experience in Marrakech that opened its doors in 1989. #travel #travelstories #blacktravel #uniquehotels 
Explore Dar Tamsna, Marrakech's first boutique hotel since 1989. Experience luxury in a unique setting. #travel #travelstories #blacktravel #uniquehotels
Dar Tamsna holds a special place in the history of hospitality in Marrakech, as it was the first boutique hotel to open its doors in 1989. Offering a unique blend of traditional Moroccan architecture and modern luxury, it set the standard for boutique accommodation in the region. Travelers seeking an unforgettable experience are drawn to its enchanting charm and personalized service. 

Located in the heart of Marrakech, Dar Tamsna is not just a hotel; it’s a home away from home, welcoming guests with an atmosphere that reflects genuine Moroccan hospitality. The hotel's design incorporates beautiful local craftsmanship, with intricate details that create a warm and inviting environment.

From its inception, Dar Tamsna has catered to the needs of diverse travelers, becoming a favorite among those looking to explore the rich culture and vibrant life of Marrakech. The hotel has earned a reputation for exceptional service, attention to detail, and unique experiences, solidifying its status as a pioneer in the boutique hotel sector.

Guests can enjoy a range of amenities, including beautifully appointed rooms, lush gardens, and exquisite dining options that celebrate Moroccan cuisine. The location provides easy access to some of Marrakech's most iconic attractions, allowing guests to immerse themselves in the local culture while enjoying the comfort of their luxurious accommodations.

As a historical landmark in the hospitality industry, Dar Tamsna continues to inspire a new generation of boutique hotels around the world. Whether you’re a seasoned traveler or a first-time visitor, your stay at Dar Tamsna promises memories that will last a lifetime. Experience the allure of Marrakech and discover why this hotel remains a top choice for unique and luxurious accommodations. 

Plan your visit to Dar Tamsna and enjoy a blend of historical significance and modern luxury. #travel #travelstories #blacktravel #uniquehotels

description: Si vous pensez connaître Marrakech, cette vidéo risque de changer votre regard. Derrière ces portes, le Maroc ne montre pas seulement un hôtel. Il montre une vision du luxe. Le @Royal Mansour Marrakech , c’est l’hospitalité marocaine dans sa forme la plus rare : des riads privés, des zelliges, des patios silencieux, un service presque invisible, et cette élégance que seuls les grands lieux possèdent. Ici, rien ne crie. Tout parle. Le bois sculpté, les lanternes, les fontaines, les jardins, les terrasses cachées… chaque détail rappelle une chose : le vrai luxe n’a pas besoin de se justifier. C’est Marrakech dans ce qu’elle a de plus noble. C’est le Maroc dans ce qu’il offre de plus grand. C’est l’art de recevoir élevé au rang de civilisation. Regardez jusqu’à la dernière image, puis envoyez cette vidéo à quelqu’un qui doit découvrir le Maroc autrement. Le Royal Mansour n’est pas seulement un palace. C’est le Maroc qui reçoit le monde. #RoyalMansour #Marrakech #LuxuryTravel #VisitMorocco

description:
Hiii here are all the details about our 3 day tour from Errachdia to Marrakech via Sahara desert.  #marrakech🇲🇦morocco #saharadesert #merzougadesert #moroccodeserttours 
Discover our 3-day tour details from Errachidia to Marrakech, including the magical Sahara and Merzouga Desert experiences. Book your Morocco desert adventure! #marrakech #saharadesert #moroccodeserttours
Embark on an unforgettable 3-day journey from Errachidia to Marrakech, traversing the breathtaking landscapes of the Sahara Desert. This meticulously planned tour offers an immersive experience in the heart of Morocco's iconic desert regions, including the famed Merzouga desert.

Your adventure begins in Errachidia, setting the stage for a captivating exploration that culminates in the vibrant city of Marrakech. Along the way, you will witness the awe-inspiring beauty of the Sahara, with its vast, undulating sand dunes and starry night skies. The Merzouga desert, known for its towering Erg Chebbi dunes, promises an authentic desert experience, complete with camel treks and traditional Berber hospitality.

This tour is designed to showcase the best of Morocco's desert charm, blending natural wonders with cultural insights. Prepare for a memorable expedition that connects you with the spirit of the Sahara and the allure of Marrakech.

description:
Marruecos - 🇲🇦 Marrakech #famimy #marrackech #marruecos 
Descubre Marrakech en Marruecos: su ambiente, calles y lugares que hacen de esta ciudad una parada imprescindible para viajeros.
Marrakech, en Marruecos, es una ciudad que llama la atención por su ambiente, sus colores y su energía. Muchos viajeros la visitan para explorar sus calles, descubrir su cultura y vivir una experiencia diferente dentro de este país del norte de África.

Marrakech destaca por su mezcla de tradición y vida cotidiana. Sus calles, plazas y mercados crean un ambiente único que atrae a quienes buscan conocer una ciudad llena de historia y cultura.

Viajar a Marrakech en Marruecos suele significar descubrir lugares emblemáticos, caminar por sus barrios y observar cómo la ciudad combina arquitectura, comercio y vida local. Es un destino que muchos incluyen en sus viajes por Marruecos.

Si estás pensando en visitar Marrakech Marruecos, esta ciudad ofrece una experiencia auténtica para quienes quieren explorar uno de los destinos más conocidos del país.

description:
Marrakech diaries 🇲🇦🪭 everything we did on day 1🫖 #marrakechvlog #marrakechitinerary #thingstodoinmarrakech #marrakechguide #marrakechshopping 
Explore Marrakech on Day 1! Discover our Riad breakfast, Dar El Bacha Museum, Jardin Majorelle, Yves Saint Laurent Museum, and dining experiences. Full Marrakech vlog highlights. #marrakechvlog #marrakechitinerary #thingstodoinmarrakech #marrakechguide #marrakechshopping
Our Marrakech diaries begin with a delightful Day 1, exploring the vibrant city. We started our morning with breakfast at our Riad, a charming and traditional Moroccan accommodation.

Our cultural exploration continued at the Dar El Bacha Museum, offering a glimpse into Moroccan arts and history. We then indulged in a coffee experience at Bacha coffee, known for its exquisite blends. For refreshments, we enjoyed juices at Palais DONAB.

Lunch was served at Sabich, followed by a matcha treat at Simple. The afternoon was dedicated to the stunning Jardin Majorelle, a botanical garden with striking blue architecture, and the nearby Yves Saint Laurent Museum, celebrating the iconic designer's work and connection to Marrakech.

Our evening concluded with dinner at La fontaine des épices, savoring the local flavors. We also teased an upcoming vlog about the souks and a shopping haul, promising more exciting content from our Marrakech adventure.

description:
The scariest part of the souks was actually all the motorcycles in the narrow streets😅🏍️😂. Have you ever been to the souks? Did you experience any scams?🤔🛍️🤷🏼‍♀️ #souks #marrakech #scams #traveltruths #travelhack 
Discover the thrills and challenges of navigating Marrakech's souks! Have you encountered scams there? Share your stories! #souks #marrakech #scams #traveltruths #travelhack
Marrakech's souks are one of the most vibrant yet overwhelming places to visit when traveling to Morocco. The bustling markets, alive with colors and sounds, can be both a thrilling and daunting experience. One of the most surprising aspects of navigating the narrow streets of the souks is the sheer number of motorcycles weaving through the crowds. This chaos adds an unexpected thrill to the shopping experience, making it a unique adventure for every traveler.

As you wander through the stalls, it's not uncommon to encounter various scams aimed at unsuspecting tourists. Many visitors share their stories about being approached by locals offering seemingly great deals, only to find out later that the prices are inflated or the products are not what they appeared to be. It's crucial to stay vigilant and informed about typical scams that happen in the area.

Have you ever been to the souks? If so, did you face any challenges or scams during your visit? It's essential to share these experiences so fellow travelers can be prepared for what to expect.

Overall, the souks of Marrakech offer an incredible glimpse into Moroccan culture and craftsmanship, but it's essential to remain cautious and aware while navigating through the excitement. If you're planning a trip, arm yourself with knowledge about the scams and stay alert while enjoying the vibrant atmosphere. Make sure to embrace all that the souks have to offer while keeping your guard up!
